_README: RelaxTouch (relaxtouchtoday.com) — Tantric Wellness Massage bootstrap (post-pivot 2026-05). 6 active studio locations. Импортируй через /seo/sites/{id}/import с галкой 'перезаписать'. Slug-based upsert обновит существующие 6 локаций (LA, Costa Mesa, Newark, Arlington, Chicago, Brooklyn) — id и контакты сохраняются. После import надо вручную удалить 9 устаревших локаций (San Francisco, Menlo Park, Riverside, Sacramento, Houston, Phoenix, Austin, San Diego, Seattle и др. — если есть).

authors.name: RelaxTouch Editorial Team
authors.bio_html: The RelaxTouch Editorial Team is a small group of experienced tantric bodyworkers and wellness writers who research, write and review every guide on this site. We draw on years of hands-on practice and a careful read of the modern bodywork literature. If you spot an error or have a topic you'd like us to cover, we'd love to hear from you.

pages.title: About RelaxTouch
pages.meta_description: RelaxTouch operates mindful tantric massage studios in six U.S. cities — quiet, modern rooms, experienced practitioners, and an hour where nothing is asked of you.
pages.content_html:
RelaxTouch is a small network of mindful tantric massage studios built for one thing: to give your body — and your nervous system — a quiet hour back.

We started with a simple observation. Most massage in the United States falls into two camps: the chain spa — fast, fluorescent, forgettable — or the medical clinic — clinical, fluorescent, also forgettable. Tantric bodywork, when it's done well, sits in a third category: slower, more attentive, more present. That's what we build.

What we mean by tantric massage

For us, tantric massage is a slow, mindful, full-body practice that treats the whole person — not just a tight muscle or a sore spot. Sessions are unhurried. Touch is intentional. The pace is set by the body in front of us, not by a script and a timer.

This is a wellness practice. Sessions are performed in a professional studio setting, in private rooms, with the comfort and ease of the person on the table as the priority. We are not a chain spa, a medical clinic, or anything else — we are a small studio that does one thing carefully.

Our practitioners

Every session at RelaxTouch is performed by an experienced tantric bodyworker from our team. Our practitioners specialise in slow touch, sensory, full-body tantric and deep body connection work, and most have trained in two or three styles, so we can match the body in front of us, not the technique we happened to learn.

Our studios

You'll find us in six U.S. cities — Los Angeles, Costa Mesa, Newark, Arlington, Chicago, and Brooklyn. Each location is independent in feel — chosen for the neighborhood, designed around its light — but every studio shares the same standards: cleanliness, discretion, premium oils and linens, and a calendar built so you're never rushed in or rushed out.

Open late, open every day

We're open 9:00 AM to midnight, seven days a week. Most of the people we serve don't have nine-to-five lives, and we don't think presence should have nine-to-five hours either.

Have a question?

The fastest way to reach a particular studio is the contact line on its page. For everything else, our central inbox is on the contact page.

pages.title: Contact RelaxTouch
pages.meta_description: Reach RelaxTouch — find direct contacts for each studio location, or the central inbox for general inquiries, press, and partnerships.
pages.content_html:
The fastest way to reach a particular studio is the phone line on its location page. For everything else — general inquiries, press, partnerships — use the central inbox below.

Studio locations

Click any studio for its direct phone line, address, and hours:

  • Los Angeles, CA
  • Costa Mesa, CA
  • Newark, CA
  • Arlington, VA
  • Chicago, IL
  • Brooklyn, NY

General inquiries

For everything that isn't a booking: hello@relaxtouchtoday.com. We read every message and reply within one business day.

Press & partnerships

Press requests, interview requests, and brand partnerships: press@relaxtouchtoday.com.

pages.title: Privacy Policy
pages.meta_description: How RelaxTouch handles your personal data — what we collect, how we use it, and your rights under CCPA and GDPR.
pages.content_html:
This Privacy Policy explains how RelaxTouch (operated by Digital Chaos Inc., 252 Seaview Ave, Staten Island, NY 10305) collects, uses and protects your personal data when you use this website or book a session.

Information we collect

When you book a session, contact a studio, or fill out a form on this site, we may collect your name, phone number, email address, and any notes you choose to share. When you simply browse the site, we collect basic analytics information (page views, device type, approximate location) through Google Analytics and similar services.

How we use it

We use this information to fulfil bookings, communicate with you about your sessions, improve the site, and comply with our legal obligations. We do not sell your personal data, and we do not share it with third parties for marketing purposes.

Your rights

If you are located in California, you have the right under the CCPA to request access to, correction of, or deletion of your personal data. If you are located in the EU or UK, you have similar rights under the GDPR. To exercise any of these rights, email privacy@relaxtouchtoday.com.

Retention

We keep booking records for as long as needed to operate the business and to meet legal and tax obligations (typically seven years). After that, records are deleted or anonymised.

Contact

Questions about this policy? Email privacy@relaxtouchtoday.com or write to Digital Chaos Inc., 252 Seaview Ave, Staten Island, NY 10305, USA.

pages.title: Terms of Service
pages.meta_description: The terms that apply when you use the RelaxTouch website or book a session at one of our studios.
pages.content_html:
These terms apply when you use the RelaxTouch website (relaxtouchtoday.com) or book a session at one of our studios. By using this site or visiting one of our locations, you agree to these terms.

Operator

RelaxTouch is operated by Digital Chaos Inc., a Domestic Business Corporation registered in the County of Richmond, NY State, with its registered address at 252 Seaview Ave, Staten Island, NY 10305, USA.

Sessions

All sessions are private, professional bodywork sessions provided in our studio locations. Sessions are non-medical. We do not diagnose, treat or claim to cure any medical condition. If you have a significant medical concern, please consult a licensed healthcare provider.

Bookings and cancellations

Bookings are confirmed by phone or email. We ask for at least 24 hours' notice for cancellations or reschedules so we can offer the time to someone else. Late cancellations and no-shows may be charged the full session fee at studio discretion.

Conduct

Sessions are conducted in a professional setting. Inappropriate behavior, requests outside the scope of a wellness session, or any conduct that makes our team feel unsafe will result in immediate end of the session without refund. We reserve the right to refuse service.

Limitation of liability

To the maximum extent permitted by law, Digital Chaos Inc. is not liable for any indirect, incidental or consequential damages arising from use of this website or any session. Our total liability is limited to the amount you paid for the relevant session.

Jurisdiction

These terms are governed by the laws of the State of New York, USA. Any dispute will be brought exclusively in the courts of Richmond County, NY.

Contact

Questions about these terms? Email hello@relaxtouchtoday.com.

locations.tagline: A quiet tantric studio in the heart of LA.

locations.intro_html: RelaxTouch Los Angeles is a small tantric massage studio built for one thing — a slower, more attentive hour. Tucked into the city's pace, our rooms are quiet, the light is soft, and the practitioner on shift takes their time. No rushed sessions, no upselling, no scripts.
locations.services_html:
Sessions

  • 60-minute — $230.
  • 30-minute — $200.

Styles we practise

  • Tantric Full Body Massage — our signature slow, intentional, full-body session.
  • Sensory Massage — lighter, attentive touch that gives the nervous system room to settle.
  • Slow Touch Massage — entry-level, unhurried — a gentle place to start if it's your first time.
  • Deep Body Connection Massage — fuller-contact bodywork for people who want a more grounded session.

Every style is offered by a practitioner trained specifically in that approach.

locations.about_studio_html:
Our Los Angeles studio is a small, private space tucked into the city's pace — close enough to the cultural arteries that you can drop in between meetings, far enough from the noise that an hour actually feels like an hour. The room is quiet. The light is low. The street outside fades the moment the door closes.

LA is one of those cities that runs on the body without ever quite admitting it. Traffic, deadlines, screens, the constant low-grade calculation of where you need to be next — none of that is dramatic on its own, but compounded over months it shows up as tight shoulders, shallow sleep, and a kind of background restlessness most people just learn to ignore. The work we do here is the opposite of all that.

Getting here

We're accessible by car from most parts of the city — Westside, Hollywood, Mid-City, DTLA — and there's reliable parking within a short walk. If you're using public transit, we're reachable from major Metro lines with a brief connecting walk. Specific directions are confirmed when you book; we've found that gives clients more useful information than a static map can.

If you're driving in from traffic, give yourself five extra minutes — not because we'll be hard to find, but because arriving rushed is the worst possible way to start a slow session. We have water and a quiet reception room. Sit for a minute before we begin.

Who we tend to see

LA brings a wide range of bodies through our door. Film and creative professionals between projects. Founders and tech workers carrying the cognitive load of a long week. Healthcare workers off a long shift. Performers, writers, people working unusual hours. What they share isn't a profession — it's the shape of a life that's been moving too fast for too long.

First-time clients are common here. LA has a higher proportion of curious-but-cautious newcomers to tantric bodywork than most cities we work in, which is why we recommend our Slow Touch or Sensory sessions as a starting point. Slower, more predictable, and easier to settle into.

Why LA, specifically

Los Angeles isn't a city that asks for your nervous system's permission. It just keeps moving, and most adults adapt by running their sympathetic system most of the day. That works — until it doesn't. Slow, full-body bodywork is one of the few interventions reliable enough to actually drop you into the other gear, the rest-and-recover gear, in a single hour. People come back not because it's a luxury but because it's the part of their week that lets the rest of the week function.

locations.about_studio_html:
Our Costa Mesa studio is a small, private space minutes from South Coast Plaza and the cultural pulse of central Orange County. The room is quiet, the parking is easy, and the door closes on a city that's always performing. For OC residents and the daily wave of South County professionals who pass through, it's a calmer way to land.

Orange County has its own pace — less frantic than LA, but with its own particular pressure: appearances, schedules, real-estate timelines, the constant gentle expectation of doing well. A slow tantric session is one of the few hours in a week here that doesn't ask anything of you.

Getting here

We're easy to reach from the 405, the 55, and the 73. Parking is free and reliable — the kind of small detail that makes the difference between arriving relaxed and arriving annoyed. South Coast Plaza, Triangle Square, and the central restaurant corridor are all within a short drive, which makes it natural to book a session before or after something else you were already doing on this side of town.

We see a steady flow of clients from Newport Beach, Irvine, Huntington Beach, and Anaheim, as well as from inside Costa Mesa itself. If you're driving in from the South County wave, give yourself a few minutes for traffic.

Who we tend to see

OC professionals — real estate, healthcare, finance, tech — make up a big share of our regulars. So do parents in the middle of busy family seasons, executives between trips, and clients in their fifties and sixties who are simply rediscovering bodywork after decades away from it. We see a fair number of couples who come separately rather than together, in adjoining-time slots; we walk through why that's usually better than a true couples session on our couples article.

New to tantric bodywork? Most Costa Mesa first-timers start with our Slow Touch or Sensory sessions — gentler, more predictable, and a fair introduction to what the slower pacing actually feels like.

Why Costa Mesa, specifically

Orange County rewards composure. It also asks for it constantly, which over years adds up to bodies that are quietly working harder than their owners realise. A slow, full-body session does for the nervous system what a vacation does — but in an hour, and without the airport. That's why people come back here. Not because it's a luxury, but because it works.

locations.about_studio_html:
Our Newark studio serves the East Bay from a small, modern space between the freeway and home — exactly where most of our clients need it. After a day spent in front of screens, in meetings, or in traffic between San Jose and Oakland, the studio is a soft landing that doesn't require detouring out of your life to reach.

The East Bay's tech corridor has a particular kind of pressure: long days, constant context-switching, and not much physical movement to discharge any of it. The body keeps the score, as the cliché goes — and the version of that score we see most often is tight shoulders, restless sleep, and a low background hum of fatigue that never quite resolves.

Getting here

We're a short drive from the 880 freeway, easy access for clients coming from Fremont, Union City, Hayward, San Leandro, Milpitas, and the south reach of Oakland. BART is reachable with a brief connection at Fremont or Union City stations — particularly useful for clients coming directly after work without going home first. Parking is free and reliable around the studio.

The Newark and Fremont corridor is well placed for the East Bay tech belt. If you're working from a Fremont, Newark, Milpitas or San Jose office, we're often the closest serious tantric studio you can reach without going into the city.

Who we tend to see

Tech workers and engineers make up a substantial share of our Newark regulars — software, hardware, semiconductors, automotive. Bay Area medicine sees us too: nurses off twelve-hour shifts, residents between rotations, healthcare administrators carrying the weight of impossible schedules. We see a meaningful number of clients in their late twenties and early thirties, which is younger than our city averages — typical of a metro built around early-career professionals.

First-time clients are very common here. Our Slow Touch session is what we recommend for people whose only previous experience is a chain spa or hotel massage; it's a gentler introduction to a much slower style of bodywork.

Why the East Bay, specifically

The East Bay tech industry is built on cognitive output. The body, in this kind of work, is mostly an inconvenience — it has to be fed, walked occasionally, and slept on demand. Over the course of years, this produces nervous systems that have forgotten how to fully downshift. A weekly or fortnightly slow tantric session reintroduces the body to its own resting gear. Most clients describe it not as relaxation but as remembering how to feel something other than alert.

locations.about_studio_html:
Our Arlington studio is a short walk from the Pentagon City corridor — close to the busiest commute in America, but designed for the moment you finally get to step out of it. The room is quiet, the entrance is discreet, and the door closes on a city that's almost always running on someone else's clock.

Arlington is shaped by the federal calendar, the military rhythm, and a constant flow of professionals who carry serious responsibility through long days. Bodywork in this kind of environment isn't a luxury — it's the part of the week that makes the rest of the week sustainable.

Getting here

We're a brief walk from the Pentagon City Metro station, which makes us reachable by Blue and Yellow lines without needing a car. Clients coming from DC proper find this particularly useful — it's a single short ride and a walk, no parking, no traffic. For drivers, we're easy from I-395, Route 1, and the GW Parkway, with reliable parking nearby.

The studio is also walkable from much of Crystal City and Pentagon City, which means a session can fit naturally into the end of a workday without a major detour. Several of our regulars come straight from offices in the corridor without going home first.

Who we tend to see

Federal employees, government contractors, military personnel between assignments, journalists, lobbyists, and DC commuters make up most of our Arlington base. We also see a steady flow of clients in healthcare and academia from the nearby hospitals and universities. Discretion is important to many of the people we work with — the studio is small, the schedule is private, and we keep the rhythm of arrivals and departures unhurried so no one is ever overlapping with anyone they'd rather not see.

For first-time clients, we typically recommend our Slow Touch or Sensory sessions. The slower pacing is a more reliable way to settle a nervous system that's been on duty for a long time.

Why Arlington, specifically

Government work — at every level — has a particular shape. Long hours, high stakes, classified or sensitive material, the constant low-level alertness that comes with carrying responsibility for things larger than yourself. The body doesn't separate that from any other kind of stress, but the nervous system runs hotter for longer than most jobs require. Slow tantric bodywork is one of the few interventions reliable enough to drop a chronically activated nervous system into actual rest in one hour. That's why we see the regulars we see, week after week.

Specific directions are sent with your booking confirmation."}, {"q": "Do you serve clients from the suburbs?", "a": "Yes — many of our regulars come from the near North Shore (Evanston, Wilmette) and the western suburbs. The studio is accessible from the major highways with reliable parking nearby."}, {"q": "Where can I park?", "a": "There's reliable parking in covered garages within a short walk — especially important from November through March. Specific recommendations are sent with your booking."}, {"q": "Which session do you recommend for winter?", "a": "Our Deep Body Connection session tends to be the most-requested in colder months — the fuller contact and slower, grounded pressure feel particularly good against indoor stiffness. Tantric Full Body is our year-round recommendation."}, {"q": "What does a first session look like?", "a": "You arrive, we offer water, you spend five to ten minutes with the practitioner — fully clothed — talking briefly about what brought you in. Then you settle on the table, draped in clean linens, and the session begins."}, {"q": "Is this a medical service?", "a": "No. RelaxTouch is a wellness practice. We don't diagnose, treat or claim to cure any medical condition."}, {"q": "How do I book?", "a": "The fastest way is the phone line on this page. We answer 9 AM to midnight, every day."}]

locations.about_studio_html:
Our Chicago studio is a small, private space — Chicago's quiet alternative to the chain spa. The room is warm, the light is low, the linens are heated, and the city's noise stops at the door. For Loop professionals, North-Side residents, and the steady flow of clients from the suburbs, it's an honest, unhurried hour in a city that mostly doesn't slow down.

Chicago is a city that asks the body to absorb a lot — long commutes, hard winters, dense schedules, the daily compromise of sitting through traffic when you'd rather be moving. The bodywork we do here is built for that. Slow, full-body, and warm.

Getting here

We're a short walk from major L lines, reachable from the Red, Brown, and Blue routes with a brief connection. The Loop, River North, and the West Loop are all within a quick ride. For drivers from the suburbs, we're accessible from the Kennedy, Eisenhower, and Lake Shore Drive, with reliable parking nearby in covered garages — particularly important between November and March when street parking gets harder.

We see regulars from Lincoln Park, Wicker Park, the West Loop, River North, Lakeview, and the near North Shore (Evanston, Wilmette), as well as commuters from the western suburbs. The studio's central position makes it a workable option for most of metro Chicago without a major detour.

Who we tend to see

Chicago brings a particular mix through our door. Finance and consulting professionals from the Loop, downtown lawyers, healthcare workers from the medical district, tech workers from the West Loop and River North, and creative professionals from the North Side neighborhoods. We see a meaningful number of clients in their forties and fifties who've reached a point in their careers where the body is no longer cooperating quite the way it used to, and who are doing something about it.

Winter is when our regulars come more often. Many clients book a session every two weeks from November through March; the warmth of the studio, the slow pacing, and the way it counteracts indoor stiffness make it a part of how people get through Chicago's heaviest months.

Why Chicago, specifically

This is a working city in a serious climate. Chicagoans tend to be tougher about it than most, which means many bodies here are carrying more tension than their owners admit to. Slow, full-body tantric bodywork does for the nervous system in winter what sunlight does for the mood — it reminds the body that not everything has to be effort. Our Deep Body Connection session is especially well-suited to colder months; the fuller contact and grounded pressure feel like an antidote to indoor stiffness.

locations.title: Tantric Massage in Brooklyn, NY
locations.tagline: The kind of quiet Brooklyn was missing.
locations.meta_description: Mindful tantric massage in Brooklyn, NY. Slow touch, sensory, full-body, and deep body connection sessions in a calm, modern studio — built for the pace of New York. Open 9 AM to midnight, daily.
locations.intro_html: RelaxTouch Brooklyn is the kind of quiet tantric studio the borough was missing — built for the pace of New York. Small, modern, unhurried. A door you close and an hour that's actually yours.
locations.services_html:
Sessions

  • 60-minute — $230 · 30-minute — $200.

Styles we practise

  • Tantric Full Body Massage · Sensory Massage · Slow Touch Massage · Deep Body Connection Massage — each offered by a practitioner trained specifically in that style.

locations.about_studio_html:
Our Brooklyn studio is the kind of quiet tantric space the borough was missing — small, modern, and built for the pace of New York. The room is silent, the light is soft, and the door closes on a city that doesn't believe in silence. For the steady flow of creative professionals, freelancers, parents, and overworked New Yorkers who pass through, it's a real hour in a borough that doesn't easily give up an hour.

Brooklyn has its own version of the New York problem. The apartments are small. The streets are loud. The day requires you to perform at full volume from the moment you step outside. Bodywork in this environment isn't a luxury; it's structural.

Getting here

We're a short walk from multiple subway lines — typically L, G, or J depending on direction — and within a reasonable ride from most of north, central, and brownstone Brooklyn. Manhattan clients reach us easily via the L from Union Square or the J from the Lower East Side. Driving from Queens or Long Island is straightforward via the BQE, with limited but workable street parking nearby.

We see regulars from Williamsburg, Greenpoint, Bushwick, Bed-Stuy, Crown Heights, Park Slope, Cobble Hill, and Carroll Gardens, as well as a meaningful number of Manhattan clients who come to Brooklyn specifically for the quieter studio environment. Exact directions are confirmed when you book.

Who we tend to see

Brooklyn brings a particular kind of body through our door. Creative professionals — designers, writers, editors, art directors — make up a big share of our regulars, along with freelancers, photographers, and people working unusual hours. We see parents who are exhausted in a specific Brooklyn way, finance professionals who commute into Manhattan but live here, and a steady flow of clients in their thirties through fifties who've concluded that the chain spa experience isn't what they actually want.

For first-timers, the slower pace can be a real change. We usually recommend our Slow Touch or Sensory sessions as a starting point; both are gentler and more predictable than the deeper modalities, and a better fit for a nervous system that's spent the day on subway alert.

Why Brooklyn, specifically

This is a city of constant input. There is nowhere in most of Brooklyn that is genuinely quiet, including most apartments. Over years, the body adapts by staying mildly alert at all times — and the cost shows up as poor sleep, shoulder tension, a kind of low-grade fatigue that never quite resolves. A slow tantric session is one of the few hours in a week where the body gets to fully release that vigilance. It's why people come back, regularly and for years.
